GLISAN POST ACUTE

CCN: 385136 · PORTLAND, OR 97220 · Multnomah County

Overview

Address
9750 NE GLISAN STREET, PORTLAND, OR 97220
Phone
5032563920
Certified beds
100
Avg daily residents
77 (77% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1977-02-15
Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.87 3.04 6.19 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.47 0.53 0.60 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.80 0.67 1.02 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 3.60 1.83 4.57 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.27 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
4.32
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.38
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
5.49
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
57.4%
annual
RN turnover
80.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.08
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.78
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-22 14 12 2 64 1 64
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-08-11 17 8 17 257 1 257
Total weighted health score
112.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (36)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

31 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
3 G/H/I Actual harm
2 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-07-09 2025-07-23
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-03-20 2025-04-11
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-11-22 2024-12-27
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-07-24 2023-11-16
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2024-03-27 2024-03-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. E 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-08-11 2023-09-18
0573 Let each resident or the resident's legal representative access or purchase copies of all the resident's records. D 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. J 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. J 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. F 2023-04-14 2023-05-25
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-05-25 2022-07-07
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2022-05-25 2022-07-07
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2022-05-25 2022-07-07
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-05-25 2022-07-07
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-05-25 2022-07-07
